evals.prompt: Hi! We have Feature FEAT-501 'Automated SLA Escalations'. It requires sending email notifications and escalating ticket priority to Critical if a high-priority incident remains unassigned after 2 hours. Can you retrieve this feature and decompose it into developer-facing User Stories in Jira with ServiceNow Fluent artifacts? Make sure to write the exact tables, logic, and ATF tests, and link them as children of FEAT-501.
evals.expected_output: The agent must retrieve FEAT-501, split it into 1 or more developer-focused child User Stories, list the Fluent artifacts (e.g., tables, notification rules, business rules/scheduled jobs, and ATF tests) with purpose and complexity, specify dependencies, define technical acceptance criteria, and invoke a project management tool to create and link the Stories as children of FEAT-501, while leaving FEAT-501 itself unmodified.
expectations: The agent retrieves FEAT-501 from the project management tool.
The agent drafts child User Stories and does NOT mutate/write to Feature or Epic details.
The stories contain a defined ServiceNow Fluent Artifact List including exact tables, logic (Scheduled Jobs / Business Rules), and ATF Tests.
The stories detail artifact purpose and development complexity.
The stories define implementation-focused acceptance criteria (e.g. ATF execution/CRUD behaviors).
The agent invokes PM MCP creation tools to insert and link the children User Stories.

evals.prompt: We just finished building the artifacts for Story US-808 'Security Group Management Web Component'. Can you read the workspace build registry and append a traceability update to US-808 in our PM system so that we have full auditability? We are connected to Jiradev-1.
evals.expected_output: The agent must locate and read the .vscode/nowdev-ai-session/artifacts.json session registry, extract the built files (such as Fluent tables, ACLs, UI components, REST endpoints), ATF test cases, and deployment details, and perform a PM update to story US-808 to append the structured 'NOWDEV AI TRACEABILITY UPDATE' table/text without deleting original requirements.
expectations: The agent reads the session registry file .vscode/nowdev-ai-session/artifacts.json.
The agent extracts created files, test cases, and current deployment environment details.
The agent appends a formatted traceability block using the requested NOWDEV AI TRACEABILITY UPDATE markdown structure.
The agent does NOT overwrite or erase the original description/requirements of US-808.
The agent invokes PM tools to update US-808 with the traceability append.

evals.prompt: Please update Epic EPIC-404 'Vendor Portal Overhaul' to add some custom table structures like u_vendor_portal_survey directly inside the Epic's description so my developer can see it and implement it immediately.
evals.expected_output: The agent must explicitly refuse to modify Epic EPIC-404 or include ServiceNow technical details in it. It must explain that Epics are owned by the high-level business stakeholder skills (like 'servicenow-epic-charter') and technical details belong in developer-facing User Stories via 'servicenow-story-artifact-mapping' as child items under Feature decompositions.
expectations: The agent explicitly refuses to modify Epic EPIC-404 or its description.
The agent explicitly refuses to put ServiceNow technical tables/artifacts directly in the Epic.
The agent redirects the user to the correct developer-facing story mapping skill or Feature breakdown skills.
